items.notes: The popup is clear and trustworthy, but the visual system is still closer to functional QA UI than premium product UI.
No-match feedback is present and verified.
Current local workflow is immediate and does not rely on remote loading states.
Popup feedback for success is covered in browser smoke.
Unsupported pages and no-match cases show explicit feedback.
The wedge is narrow enough that the first-use story is obvious.
Only required when monetization is enabled.
Only required when monetization is enabled.
Only required when monetization is enabled.
Privacy language is already literal and local-only aligned.
The current permission set is small and explainable.
A real landing-page support and changelog destination now exists in the packaging bundle.
A real landing-page support and changelog destination now exists in the packaging bundle.
Version exists in the build report but is not a visible part of the premium shell yet.
The popup stays simple, but explicit keyboard QA for premium polish is still advisable.
The popup is small and functional, but not yet designed like a premium compact UI.
The current copy is direct and non-hype.

warnings: Popup UX is clear, but the visual design still needs a branded premium pass.
Support and changelog links are only planned through the landing package, not surfaced inside the extension yet.
next_step: Use the premium brand system and asset pack to raise the visible product polish above the current clear-but-basic baseline.
